title: The Self Beyond Division
summary: Explains why the Self cannot be divided or defined by scriptures. Addresses confusion about religions, sects, and spiritual paths.

sections.summary: Themes: devotion, mind and meditation, knowledge and discernment. The passage moves from “Do not think of those who are immature, hedonistic, idiots, servants, householders etc. as inferior. Who throws away a gem that has gone dirty?” through “If there exists such a simple and natural reality, how can I be there? How can you or all living and inert objects be there?” through “Knowledge of external world is illusory. Knowledge of internal cause is the right one. Know what lies inside that interior, like water in the…”

Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “Consciousness is one, like a full moon in the night, one and very pure. Seeing duality is an error.” through “One becomes pure, beyond comparison, formless, independent, bodiless, desireless, has no conflicts, is detached, and gets everlasting powers,…” through “Where mind and speech is useless, Guru’s teachings say nothing. How can a Guru tell about that Truth which reveals itself?”

verses.sanskrit: बालस्य वा विषयभोगरतस्य वापि मूर्खस्य सेवकजनस्य गृहस्थितस्य । एतद्गुरोः किमपि नैव न चिन्तनीयं रत्नं कथं त्यजति कोऽप्यशुचौ प्रविष्टम् ।। १।।
verses.translation: Do not think of those who are immature, hedonistic, idiots, servants, householders etc. as inferior. Who throws away a gem that has gone dirty?

verses.sanskrit: नैवात्र काव्यगुण एव तु चिन्तनीयो ग्राह्यः परं गुणवता खलु सार एव। सिन्दूरचित्ररहिता भुवि रूपशून्या पारं न किं नयति नौरिह गन्तुकामान्।। २।।
verses.translation: Do not consider one’s scholarly qualities or oratory skills as important, get the essence of everything. Wouldn’t a well decorated and painted boat takes passengers across, just like a simple boat does?

verses.sanskrit: प्रयत्नेन विना येन निश्चलेन चलाचलम्। ग्रस्तं स्वभावतः शान्तं चैतन्यं गगनोपमम्।।३।।
verses.translation: All that is moving and stationary is pervaded by naturally peaceful and unmoving Consciousness, like space, without any efforts.

verses.sanskrit: अयत्नाच्चालयेद्यस्तु एकमेव चराचरम्। सर्वगं तत्कथं भिन्नमद्वैतं वर्तते मम।।४।।
verses.translation: The one and only that without any effort exists everywhere and goes everywhere, how can that be differentiated? It appears non-dual to me.

verses.sanskrit: अहमेव परं यस्मात्सारासारतरं शिवम्। गमागमविनिर्मुक्तं निर्विकल्पं निराकुलम्।।५।।
verses.translation: I am the only one who is ultimate, the essence of all essences, the Consciousness. Free from transmigration, one and the only, calm and quiet.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वावयवनिर्मुक्तं तथाहं त्रिदशार्चितम्। सम्पूर्णत्वान्न गृह्णामि विभागं त्रिदशादिकम्।।६।।
verses.translation: Not having any parts or divisions, I am worshipped by the thirty. However, being one and complete, I do not discriminate the thirty etc.

verses.sanskrit: प्रमादेन न सन्देहः किं करिष्यामि वृत्तिमान्। उत्पद्यन्ते विलीयन्ते बुद्बुदाश्च यथा जले।।७।।
verses.translation: What can one do, the activities of the mind give rise to ignorance, inertia and doubts. They appear and disappear like bubble in water.

verses.sanskrit: महदादीनि भूतानि समाप्यैवं सदैव हि। मृदुद्रव्येषु तीक्ष्णेषु गुडेषु कटुकेषु च।।८।।
verses.translation: Consciousness pervades the first substance and all physical elements always. Soft or hard, sweet or bitter.

verses.sanskrit: कटुत्वं चैव शैत्यत्वं मृदुत्वं च यथा जले। प्रकृतिः पुरुषस्तद्वदभिन्नं प्रतिभाति मे।।९।।
verses.translation: Just as the qualities of sweetness or bitterness, and coldness or fluidity cannot be differentiated from the water that has those, I do not see the existence and Consciousness as different.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वाख्यारहितं यद्यत्सूक्ष्मात्सूक्ष्मतरं परम्। मनोबुद्धीन्द्रियातीतमकलङ्कं जगत्पतिम्।।१०।।
verses.translation: Consciousness, the ruler of all universes, is beyond all descriptions, subtler than the subtlest, it is the ultimate, spotlessly clean, beyond the mind, intellect and senses.

verses.sanskrit: ईदृशं सहजं यत्र अहं तत्र कथं भवेत्। त्वमेव हि कथं तत्र कथं तत्र चराचरम्।।११।।
verses.translation: If there exists such a simple and natural reality, how can I be there? How can you or all living and inert objects be there?

verses.sanskrit: गगनोपमं तु यत्प्रोक्तं तदेव गगनोपमम्। चैतन्यं दोषहीनं च सर्वज्ञं पूर्णमेव च।।१२।।
verses.translation: That which is said to be like the metaphorical empty space is exactly that. Aware, perfect, all knowing and complete.

verses.sanskrit: पृथिव्यां चरितं नैव मारुतेन च वाहितम्। वारिणा पिहितं नैव तेजोमध्ये व्यवस्थितम्।।१३।।
verses.translation: It does not walk on the earth, does not get carried away by the wind, does not drown in water, and is not located in the fire too.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशं तेन संव्याप्तं न तद्व्याप्तं च केनचित्। स बाह्याभ्यन्तरं तिष्ठत्यवच्छिन्नं निरन्तरम्।।१४।।
verses.translation: Space is pervaded by it, but it is not pervaded by anything. It does not exist inside or outside of anything. It is undivided, eternal and continuous.

verses.sanskrit: सूक्ष्मत्वात्तददृश्यत्वान्निर्गुणत्वाच्च योगिभिः। आलम्बनादि यत्प्रोक्तं क्रमादालम्बनं भवेत्।।१५।।
verses.translation: As mentioned by yogis, one should concentrate on the micro, the invisible and the attribute-less, in that order.

verses.sanskrit: सतताऽभ्यासयुक्तस्तु निरालम्बो यदा भवेत्। तल्लयाल्लीयते नान्तर्गुणदोषविवर्जितः।।१६।।
verses.translation: After continuous practice, when one’s mind concentrates on almost nothing specific, one dissolves into the Consciousness, being devoid of all qualities.

verses.sanskrit: विषविश्वस्य रौद्रस्य मोहमूर्च्छाप्रदस्य च। एकमेव विनाशाय ह्यमोघं सहजामृतम्।।१७।।
verses.translation: In order to destroy the poisonous effects of this violent worldly illusion, which produces unConsciousness and clinging, being Consciousness simply and naturally, is the best remedy.

verses.sanskrit: भावगम्यं निराकारं साकारं दृष्टिगोचरम्। भावाभावविनिर्मुक्तमन्तरालं तदुच्यते।।१८।।
verses.translation: The formless can be felt in the mind, the forms can be seen with the eyes. That which is beyond form and formlessness, is called a vacancy.

verses.sanskrit: बाह्यभावं भवेद्विश्वमन्तः प्रकृचिरुच्यते। अन्तरादन्तरं ज्ञेयं नारिकेलफलाम्बुवत्।।१९।।
verses.translation: The world exists externally to what is called Prakriti. Know that which in even inside it. Just like the water is inside the pulp of a coconut and the pulp in turn is inside the hard shell.

verses.sanskrit: भ्रान्तिज्ञानं स्थितं बाह्यं सम्यग्ज्ञानं च मध्यगम्। मध्यान्मध्यतरं ज्ञेयं नारिकेलफलाम्बुवत्।।२०।।
verses.translation: Knowledge of external world is illusory. Knowledge of internal cause is the right one. Know what lies inside that interior, like water in the innermost cavity of a coconut.

verses.sanskrit: पौर्णमास्यां यथा चन्द्र एक एवातिनिर्मलः। तेन तत्सदृशं पश्येद्द्विधादृष्टिर्विपर्ययः।।२१।।
verses.translation: Consciousness is one, like a full moon in the night, one and very pure. Seeing duality is an error.

verses.sanskrit: अनेनैव प्रकारेण बुद्धिभेदो न सर्वगः। दाता च धीरतामेति गीयते नामकोटिभिः।।२२।।
verses.translation: Because of various reasons the omnipresent one is beyond intellect. Givers of this knowledge sing a million songs for it patiently.

verses.sanskrit: गुरुप्रज्ञाप्रसादेन मूर्खो वा यदि पण्डितः। यस्तु संबुध्यते तत्त्वं विरक्तो भवसागरात्।।२३।।
verses.translation: Through the gracious gift of this knowledge by a Guru, idiots as well as pundits equally understand this Truth and become disillusioned and detached from this ocean of existence.

verses.sanskrit: रागद्वेषविनिर्मुक्तः सर्वभूतहिते रतः। दृढबोधश्च धीरश्च स गच्छेत्परमं पदम्।।२४।।
verses.translation: One who is free from attachments and hate, who is involved in uplifting everyone, who has very certain knowledge, who has patience, he will attain the highest post.

verses.sanskrit: घटे भिन्ने घटाकाश आकाशे लीयते यथा। देहाभावे तथा योगी स्वरूपे परमात्मनि।।२५।।
verses.translation: A yogi merges into the universal Consciousness after leaving the body, just like the space inside a clay pot merges with all space once it is broken.

verses.sanskrit: उक्तेयं कर्मयुक्तानां मतिर्यान्तेऽपि सा गतिः। न चोक्ता योगयुक्तानां मतिर्यान्तेऽपि सा गतिः।।२६।।
verses.translation: It is said that the future of those who are engaged in worldly activities is determined by their state of mind at the time of their death. This is not said for the yogis.

verses.sanskrit: या गतिः कर्मयुक्तानां सा च वागिन्द्रियाद्वदेत्। योगिनां या गतिः क्वापि ह्यकथ्या भवतोर्जिता।।२७।।
verses.translation: One can speak of/predict the destiny of those who are involved in worldly actions. It is not possible to do the same for yogis.

verses.sanskrit: एवं ज्ञात्वा त्वमुं मार्गं योगिनां नैव कल्पितम्। विकल्पवर्जनं तेषां स्वयं सिद्धिः प्रवर्तते।।२८।।
verses.translation: Knowing this, one cannot imagine that yogis travel a destined path. Having no ignorance, for them, self-perfection happens on its own.

verses.sanskrit: तीर्थे वान्त्यजगेहे वा यत्र कुत्र मृतोऽपि वा। न योगी पश्यते गर्भं परे ब्रह्मणि लीयते।।२९।।
verses.translation: The yogi, whether he dies at a holy place or anywhere else, never sees a womb again, he is dissolved in the universal Consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: सहजमजमचिन्त्यं यस्तु पश्येत्स्वरूपं घटति यदि यथेष्टं लिप्यते नैव दोषैः। सकृदपि तदभावात्कर्म किञ्चिन्नकुर्यात् तदपि न च विबद्धः संयमी वा तपस्वी।।३०।।
verses.translation: Having seen himself as most natural and simple, unborn and beyond intellect, he never suffers from any faults due to desires. In that state he acts without acting. A self-restrained practitioner is hence, never bound by anything.

verses.sanskrit: निरामयं निष्प्रतिमं निराकृतिं निराश्रयं निर्वपुषं निराशिषम्। निर्द्वन्द्वनिर्मोहमलुप्तशक्तिकं तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३१।।
verses.translation: One becomes pure, beyond comparison, formless, independent, bodiless, desireless, has no conflicts, is detached, and gets everlasting powers, when he attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: वेदो न दीक्षा न च मुण्डनक्रिया गुरुर्न शिष्यो न च यन्त्रसम्पदः। मुद्रादिकं चापि न यत्र भासते तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३२।।
verses.translation: It is not done through Vedas, nor initiation, not by shaving your head, not via a Guru, not by being a student, not by wealth and worldly techniques, not by yogic postures, or not by wearing ashes.

verses.sanskrit: न शाम्भवं शाक्तिकमानवं न वा पिण्डं च रूपं च पदादिकं न वा। आरम्भनिष्पत्तिघटादिकं च नो तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३३।।
verses.translation: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness not via Shambhavi, nor Kundalini, nor Mantras, not by worshipping forms and figures, not by worshipping feet or footprints and not by accomplishment of rituals or ceremonies.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य स्वरूपात्सचराचरं जगद् उत्पद्यते तिष्ठति लीयतेऽपि वा। पयोविकारादिव फेनबुद्बुदा- स्तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३४।।
verses.translation: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness by realizing That which gives rise to the whole universe with its all living and inert forms, which stay there for a while and dissolve back just like foam and bubble arise out of water and dissolve back into it.

verses.sanskrit: नासानिरोधो न च दृष्टिरासनं बोधोऽप्यबोधोऽपि न यत्र भासते। नाडीप्रचारोऽपि न यत्र किञ्चित् तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३५।।
verses.translation: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness not by restraining one’s breath, not by concentrating one’s gaze, not by refining one’s nervous system. In him there is no knowledge or ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: नानात्वमेकत्वमुभत्वमन्यता अणुत्वदीर्घत्वमहत्त्वशून्यता। मानत्वमेयत्वसमत्ववर्जितं तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।।३६।।
verses.translation: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness by realizing that which is not many, which is not one, which has no sameness or otherness, which is not tiny or huge, which is not the first substance or nothingness, which cannot be known by any external means, which cannot be objectively measured and which has no similarity with anything else.

verses.sanskrit: सुसंयमी वा यदि वा न संयमी सुसंग्रही वा यदि वा न संग्रही। निष्कर्मको वा यदि वा सकर्मक स्तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्तम्।।३७।।
verses.translation: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness by not disciplining one’s mind or being undisciplined, not by controlling one’s senses or letting go of them, not by being a doer or a non-doer.

verses.sanskrit: मनो न बुद्धिर्न शरीरमिन्द्रियं तन्मात्रभूतानि न भूतपञ्चकम्। अहंकृतिश्चापि वियत्स्वरूपकं तमीशमात्मानमुपैति शाश्वतम्।३८।।
verses.translation: One attains the state of highest eternal Consciousness by realizing that which is not mind, not intellect, not body, not senses, not non-physical, not physical, not ego, and not even any exotic energetic form of some kind.

verses.sanskrit: विधौ निरोधे परमात्मतां गते न योगिनश्चेतसि भेदवर्जिते। शौचं न वाशौचमलिङ्गभावना सर्वं विधेयं यदि वा निषिध्यते।।३९।।
verses.translation: Upon realizing the ultimate universal Consciousness, a yogi goes beyond restrictions of spiritual practices, he sees no duality or differences of any kind. He has no concern with being of a particular gender or being pure or impure. Upon knowing the ultimate Truth, nothing is prohibited for a yogi.

verses.sanskrit: मनो वचो यत्र न शक्तमीरितुं नूनं कथं तत्र गुरूपदेशता। इमां कथामुक्तवतो गुरोस्तद् युक्तस्य तत्त्वं हि समं प्रकाशते।।४०।।
verses.translation: Where mind and speech is useless, Guru’s teachings say nothing. How can a Guru tell about that Truth which reveals itself?
